metadata.description: Test home page for 78th Street Studios generated from the current live site content.

components.headline: Northeast Ohio's eclectic arts maze
components.subheadline: A generated test homepage shaped from the current live site, centered on monthly art nights, studio discovery, and event-ready industrial space.

items.title: Large creative campus
items.body: The live site describes 170,000 square feet of galleries, studios, performance space, and music recording rooms inside one Cleveland complex.

items.title: Deep mix of tenants
items.body: The public-facing pitch highlights more than 60 galleries, artist studios, and creative businesses working under one roof.

items.title: Third Fridays draw people in
items.body: Once a month, the building opens across multiple floors for exhibitions, music, food, and vendors in a single shared event window.

items.title: Third Fridays
items.body: The current about page says the public event runs every third Friday from 5 to 9 pm.

items.title: Cleveland location
items.body: The site lists the address as 1300 West 78th Street in Cleveland, Ohio 44102.

items.title: More than art viewing
items.body: The venue also promotes private rentals, wedding and event hosting, and year-round use of its industrial spaces.

components.headline: See the building history and who works inside
components.body: The about page is where the current site explains the building story, the rise of Third Fridays, and the tenant mix.

metadata.description: Test about page for 78th Street Studios generated from the current live site content.

components.headline: A century-old building turned creative hub
components.subheadline: This version condenses the current site history into a simpler page about an early auto factory, the American Greetings years, and the present-day arts campus.

components.title: What the current site says 78th Street Studios is

items.title: Arts and design complex
items.body: The live about page presents the building as the largest art and design complex in Northeast Ohio with galleries, studios, performance areas, and recording space.

items.title: Monthly public event
items.body: Third Fridays is framed as the signature recurring event, bringing visual exhibits, music, food, and pop-up vendors together across the building.

items.title: Private event venue
items.body: Outside of its own programming, the site says spaces throughout the building are rented for private functions.

items.question: How did the building start?
items.answer: The current site says the structure dates to 1905 and originally housed the Baker Electric Motor Vehicle Company.

items.question: What came after the auto era?
items.answer: It later became home to American Greetings' creative studios before evolving into today's arts-focused tenant mix.

items.question: Why does Third Fridays matter?
items.answer: The site describes it as a small early-2000s gathering that grew into a monthly arts destination and then into a wider district event.

components.title: Who the about page highlights inside the building

items.title: Art studios
items.body: The tenant list starts with working artists and studio practices spread across the building.

items.title: Art galleries
items.body: Multiple gallery tenants are named as anchors for visitors who want to browse exhibitions.

items.title: Design services
items.body: The current site groups conservation, framing, publishing, and other design-led businesses together.

items.title: Photo and film
items.body: Studios for photography and film production are presented as part of the broader creative ecosystem.

items.title: Business services
items.body: The building also hosts business tenants, not just arts-focused occupants.

items.title: Entertainment and events
items.body: Recording, theatre, publishing, and event-oriented groups round out the mix described on the site.

components.headline: Use the live site for full tenant lists and event details
components.body: This generated version is intentionally compressed. The live site still carries the directory, maps, and current event details.
